name: Agricultural Supply Shock
description: Disruptions to fertilizer, grain, or crop supply that drive commodity prices and benefit agri-commodity producers

themes.name: Fertilizer Supply Chain Disruption · Global Repricing

reasoning: This archetype spans two distinct mechanisms: (1) fertilizer/input supply disruptions affecting production costs, and (2) grain/crop output supply shocks affecting food commodity prices. These have different causal chains, affected companies, and market dynamics.

split_suggestions.name: Fertilizer Supply Shock
split_suggestions.description: Disruptions to fertilizer supply (potash, phosphate, nitrogen) that increase input costs for farmers and benefit fertilizer producers.

split_suggestions.name: Grain and Crop Supply Shock
split_suggestions.description: Disruptions to grain and crop output supply that drive food commodity prices and benefit grain producers/traders.

name: Crypto Institutional Adoption
description: Bitcoin ETF flows, stablecoin regulation, corporate BTC treasury moves, or major institutional crypto adoption

themes.name: Trump Crypto Policy Volatility · BTC Price Swings

reasoning: This archetype conflates at least four distinct mechanisms: ETF/investment products, regulatory frameworks for stablecoins, corporate treasury decisions, and crypto infrastructure licensing. Each operates through different actors, timelines, and market dynamics.

split_suggestions.name: Crypto ETF & Investment Products
split_suggestions.description: Bitcoin ETFs, crypto spot ETFs, institutional investment vehicle approvals and their associated fund flows.

split_suggestions.name: Stablecoin Regulation & Infrastructure
split_suggestions.description: Regulatory frameworks, payment licenses, and infrastructure development specific to stablecoins and payment tokens.

split_suggestions.name: Corporate Bitcoin Treasury Adoption
split_suggestions.description: Public companies adding Bitcoin to balance sheets as treasury reserve asset or corporate strategy shifts.

split_suggestions.name: Crypto Exchange & Custody Infrastructure
split_suggestions.description: Institutional crypto custody solutions, exchange licensing, and digital asset infrastructure development.

split_suggestions.name: Crypto Regulatory Policy & SEC Actions
split_suggestions.description: Broad SEC regulatory decisions, crypto classification frameworks, and federal crypto policy developments.

name: Defense Buildup & Military Contracts
description: Increased defense spending, major weapons contracts, or NATO frontier buildup

themes.name: Iran War Munitions Replenishment · Patriot Missile
Japan Defense Export Liberalization · Production Ramp
Combat Rescue Helicopter Upgrade · IR Countermeasures

reasoning: The archetype conflates distinct mechanisms: government budget allocation for defense spending, commercial contracts between governments and defense contractors, and geopolitical military buildups. These represent different trigger events (fiscal policy vs procurement vs deployment) that could be tracked separately.

split_suggestions.name: Defense Budget & Appropriations
split_suggestions.description: Government announcements of defense budget increases, military spending bills, or national defense appropriations.

split_suggestions.name: Military Procurement & Weapons Contracts
split_suggestions.description: Awards of contracts for weapons systems, military equipment, or defense platforms to contractors.

split_suggestions.name: Foreign Military Sales & Arms Exports
split_suggestions.description: Government-to-government arms deals, foreign military sales approvals, or defense export agreements.

split_suggestions.name: Military Deployment & Force Posture
split_suggestions.description: Announcements of troop deployments, base expansions, or military buildups in strategic regions.

name: Energy Transition Acceleration · AI Power Demand / Nuclear Renaissance
description:
Explosive power demand from 
  AI data centers, nuclear technology breakthroughs (SMR commercialization), and large-scale energy storage deployment are triggering a revaluation of     
  clean energy infrastructure, grid modernization assets, and nuclear supply chains.

themes.name: AI Data Center Power · Semiconductor Efficiency

reasoning: This archetype combines three distinct mechanisms: (1) AI-driven power demand creating capacity constraints, (2) nuclear technology renaissance with SMR commercialization, and (3) energy storage deployment. Each has different drivers, stakeholders, and market dynamics that deserve separate tracking.

split_suggestions.name: AI Data Center Power Demand
split_suggestions.description: Explosive electricity consumption from AI compute infrastructure driving power purchase agreements, grid capacity investments, and utility revaluations.

split_suggestions.name: Nuclear Renaissance · SMR Commercialization
split_suggestions.description: Small modular reactor deployment, nuclear supply chain expansion, and regulatory approvals revitalizing nuclear energy as clean baseload power.

split_suggestions.name: Grid Modernization · Energy Storage
split_suggestions.description: Large-scale battery storage deployment, transmission infrastructure upgrades, and grid capacity expansion enabling renewable integration and reliability.

split_suggestions.name: Clean Energy Corporate Offtake
split_suggestions.description: Long-term power purchase agreements from corporations driving renewable project financing and clean energy infrastructure build-out.

name: Middle East Conflict · Oil War Premium
description:
When military conflicts or escalating sanctions occur in the 
  Middle East region (involving Iran, Israel, Red Sea/Strait of Hormuz), crude oil supply faces threats, causing oil prices to surge with a war premium and
   energy stocks to outperform.

themes.name: Iran Crisis Escalation · Oil War Premium

reasoning: This archetype covers a single specific mechanism: geopolitical conflict in the Middle East disrupting oil supply routes and production, creating a war risk premium in energy markets. All trigger keywords relate to the same geographic region and the same causal chain (conflict → supply threat → oil price spike).

name: U.S.-China Tariff Escalation · Domestic Substitution
description:
Rising U.S. tariffs on Chinese semiconductors, 
  critical technologies, and strategic materials—combined with expanded export controls—trigger domestic substitution demand and upward revaluation of     
  upstream critical material suppliers in China.

themes.name: Allied Chip Export Controls · China Localization

reasoning: This archetype conflates the trigger mechanism (U.S. tariffs/export controls) with the response mechanism (China domestic substitution) and covers multiple distinct policy instruments (tariffs, entity lists, export controls) and multiple affected sectors (semiconductors, critical materials, broader technology). These represent separate causal chains that should be tracked independently.

split_suggestions.name: U.S. Semiconductor Export Controls · China
split_suggestions.description: U.S. restrictions on advanced chip exports, semiconductor manufacturing equipment, and related technology transfers to China drive localization efforts in Chinese semiconductor supply chain.

split_suggestions.name: U.S.-China Tariff Imposition · Import Cost
split_suggestions.description: Direct impact of U.S. tariff increases on Chinese goods raising import costs, reducing demand for Chinese exports, or triggering retaliatory tariffs.

split_suggestions.name: China Critical Materials Localization
split_suggestions.description: Chinese domestic substitution demand for rare earths, strategic minerals, and upstream materials due to supply chain security concerns drives revaluation of local suppliers.

split_suggestions.name: U.S. Entity List · Technology Access Restriction
split_suggestions.description: Addition of Chinese firms to U.S. Entity List or similar restricted parties lists curtails their access to U.S. technology, components, and software.

split_suggestions.name: U.S.-China Technology Decoupling · Supply Chain
split_suggestions.description: Broad strategic decoupling initiatives forcing bifurcation of technology supply chains, standards, and ecosystems between U.S. and Chinese spheres.
